mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decrease in the effective federal funds rate lowers the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, which mechanically increases present equity valuations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following month-over-month FEDFUNDS declines are not positive on average over a 3-year rolling window, or the t-statistic of the mean return difference versus flat-rate months falls below 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate of more than 0.3 percentage points within a three-month window signals deteriorating household income expectations and falling aggregate demand, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day forward returns in more than 50 percent of instances when the trigger fires, or the rank correlation between UNRATE change and 60-day returns is positive over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate coordinated weakness in consumer spending and residential investment, which jointly compresses top-line revenues and forward earnings estimates, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of declining RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce negative average 20-day returns over a 3-year rolling window, or the interaction term adds no incremental explanatory power in a regression versus individual proxies (F-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in industrial production predicts positive S&P 500 returns when the federal funds rate exceeds 2 percent, because supply resilience during restrictive monetary periods signals sustainable earnings rather than inflationary overheating.
failure_condition: During periods with FEDFUNDS above 2.0%, positive INDPRO changes are followed by negative 60-day returns in more than 45 percent of observations, or the mean forward return conditional on the trigger is not statistically greater than zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the consumer price index above 0.5 percent signals sticky inflation that forces the Federal Reserve to tighten policy faster than discounted by markets, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are positive in more than 55 percent of months when CPIAUCSL rises more than 0.5 percent, or the mean return conditional on the trigger is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Joint increases in total nonfarm payrolls and retail sales confirm that both income and expenditure channels of aggregate demand are expanding, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint increase in PAYEMS and RSAFS fails to produce positive average 20-day returns over a 3-year rolling window, or a regression shows no significant interaction effect between the two proxies on forward returns (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production of more than 1 percent signals acute supply chain contraction and negative operating leverage, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following month-over-month INDPRO declines greater than 1 percent are positive in more than 50 percent of instances, or the mean conditional return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating growth in nonfarm payrolls predicts positive S&P 500 returns when the unemployment rate is below 4 percent, because payroll gains in a tight labor market reflect strong demand rather than recovery from slack.
failure_condition: When UNRATE is below 4.0%, positive PAYEMS changes are followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 45 percent of observations, or the rank correlation between PAYEMS change and 60-day returns is negative over any 3-year rolling window.
